title: Brickmasons and Blockmasons
description: Lay and bind building materials, such as brick, structural tile, concrete block, cinder block, glass block, and terra-cotta block, with mortar and other substances, to construct or repair walls, partitions, arches, sewers, and other structures.

tasks.text: Measure distance from reference points and mark guidelines to lay out work, using plumb bobs and levels.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Construct corners by fastening in plumb position a corner pole or building a corner pyramid of bricks, and filling in between the corners using a line from corner to corner to guide each course, or layer, of brick.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Apply and smooth mortar or other mixture over work surface.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Calculate angles and courses and determine vertical and horizontal alignment of courses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Break or cut bricks, tiles, or blocks to size, using trowel edge, hammer, or power saw.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Interpret blueprints and drawings to determine specifications and to calculate the materials required.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove excess mortar with trowels and hand tools, and finish mortar joints with jointing tools, for a sealed, uniform appearance.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fasten or fuse brick or other building material to structure with wire clamps, anchor holes, torch, or cement.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean working surface to remove scale, dust, soot, or chips of brick and mortar, using broom, wire brush, or scraper.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine brickwork or structure to determine need for repair.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mix specified amounts of sand, clay, dirt, or mortar powder with water to form refractory mixtures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove burned or damaged brick or mortar, using sledgehammer, crowbar, chipping gun, or chisel.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lay and align bricks, blocks, or tiles to build or repair structures or high temperature equipment, such as cupola, kilns, ovens, or furnaces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Spray or spread refractory material over brickwork to protect against deterioration.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
